texto: I am in awe of the perpetual tumult of the sea. I am moved by the still place on the horizon where the sky begins. I am stirred by the soaring and dipping fields that make the landscape into a rumpled green counterpane. I thought I would never have such powerful feelings again. I thought I would live through the rest of my life having experiences, and thoughts, but I never thought I would again feel deeply-- I was convinced that my wounds had healed and become thick scars, essentially numb.
explicacao: Citação de Katharine Weber sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “I am in awe of the perpetual tumult of the sea. I am moved by the still place on the horizon wher…” articula vida, pain, sea em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como pain e sea.

frase_original: I am in awe of the perpetual tumult of the sea. I am moved by the still place on the horizon where the sky begins. I am stirred by the soaring and dipping fields that make the landscape into a rumpled green counterpane. I thought I would never have such powerful feelings again. I thought I would live through the rest of my life having experiences, and thoughts, but I never thought I would again feel deeply-- I was convinced that my wounds had healed and become thick scars, essentially numb.

texto: But when I say it isn't meant for anyone's eyes, I don't mean it in the sense of one of those novel manuscripts people keep in a drawer, insisting they don't care if anyone else ever reads it or not.The people I have known who do that, I am convinced, have no faith in themselves as writers and know, deep down, that the novel is flawed, that they don't know how to tell the story, or they don't understand what the story is, or they haven't really got a story to tell. The manuscript in the drawer is the story.

texto: Life seems sometimes like nothing more than a series of losses, from beginning to end. That's the given. How you respond to those losses, what you make of what's left, that's the part you have to make up as you go.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Katharine Weber: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Life seems sometimes like nothing more than a series of losses, from beginning to end. That's the…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, loss, losses pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e luto.
